title: Mini accordion photo album
subtitle: A fold-out strip album for a series of small photographs or cards
excerpt: An accordion photo album is a strip of heavy paper folded into equal panels, with each panel holding one photograph or card. It stands up on a table as a continuous display or folds flat for posting in an envelope. This tutorial makes a 6-panel album with optional hard covers from a single strip of 220 gsm cartridge.
sourceNotes: Esther K. Smith, How to Make Books (2007, Potter Craft), accordion chapter. The mini album variant with optional board covers follows standard craft-book construction practice.

content.text: structure is among the fastest to make from a single sheet. The only precision required is in the fold alignment: if the fold lines are not parallel and evenly spaced, the panels don't sit flat when closed.

For a 6-panel album with A6-landscape (148 × 105 mm) panels: cut a strip of 220 gsm cartridge 110 mm tall × (148 mm × 6 + 20 mm joining allowance) = 108 mm × 908 mm. If your paper is not long enough, make a
: cut two pieces and overlap 10 mm at a panel-fold position, gluing firmly with PVA and pressing flat. The join must land exactly on a fold line.

Mark the panel divisions with a pencil: every 148 mm along the strip. Score lightly with the bone folder tip against a metal ruler. Fold the first score as a valley fold (toward you), the second as a mountain fold (away from you), alternating. Press each fold firmly with the bone folder. Lay the strip face-down on the table and press it flat under a book for 10 minutes.

For optional hard covers, cut two pieces of 1.5 mm board to panel size. Cover each with a piece of decorative paper or book cloth, turning in 15 mm at all four edges. Glue the first panel of the accordion strip flat against the inside of the front cover; glue the last panel against the inside of the back cover. Press both under boards for 20 minutes each.

Fit photographs to each panel using photo corners at all four corners. Cut photographs to a size that leaves at least 10 mm margin at all edges of the panel. The photo corners allow photographs to be replaced or updated without damaging the accordion album itself. A small caption card slipped under the bottom photo corners, or written on the back of each panel, records the date and event.
